$comment: HestiaRE installer manifest - version 1 target architecture. This is a specification (target state), not necessarily the current implementation status. Read by install.sh to drive the interactive question catalog. Re-check against actually available repo versions on every release refresh (see the 'dynamic' fields).
$parser: jq (not python3 - too heavyweight just for JSON parsing). install.sh must check/install jq as the very first dependency, before the whiptail check, since the manifest must be read before every question. jq is ~113 KB installed size + libjq1/libonig5, much leaner than a python3 dependency.

software_versions.$comment: Release-shipped third-party version pins (#288, replaces install/upgrade/upgrade.conf). Bump per release. Read at runtime via manifest_get() in func/main.sh.

standard.label: Standard (full hosting + full mail + DB + MultiPHP + addons)

latest.label: Latest (newest versions, external repos for web/PHP/DB)

singlephp.label: SinglePHP (OS PHP instead of Sury, lean setup)

nomail.label: No-Mail (hosting only, no mail block, Exim send-only)

mailonly.label: Mail-Only (mail stack only, no customer web server)

pre_questions.question: Panel port:
pre_questions.note: Asked in every preset, including standard.
No interactive prompt. Generated randomly (openssl rand) by h-install-hestia (configure stage) at the end and printed once. Not stored in install.conf, no cleartext parameter pass-through.

group_questions.db: Which database & cache services to install? (multiple choice)

WEB_SERVER.question: Web server:
WEB_SERVER.note: Fixed to NGINX on mailonly, not asked (Roundcube reverse proxy + ACME termination). Repo source (OS vs. Sury Apache / nginx.org) follows repo_strategy, see WEB_REPO_SOURCE.

WEB_REPO_SOURCE.question: Web server package source:
WEB_REPO_SOURCE.description: OS package vs. external upstream repo (Sury-Apache, nginx.org) for Apache/nginx. Normally not a user question, fully determined by the preset. Exception: under the 'custom' preset (default null) it is asked as a real question.

PHP_MODE.question: PHP mode:
PHP_MODE.description: Architecture pre-question: Single-PHP (OS package, no Sury) vs. MultiPHP (Sury repo). Not asked when fixed by the preset (singlephp, mailonly); otherwise derived from the preset. Exception: under the 'custom' preset (default null) it is asked as a real question.
PHP_MODE.note: On mailonly, Panel-PHP also uses os_single (no additional PHP beyond Panel-PHP).

PHP_MULTIPHP_VERSIONS.question: Which PHP versions to install?
PHP_MULTIPHP_VERSIONS.note: install.sh must add the Sury repo as an APT source before this question (incl. apt-get update) to derive the option list via apt-cache madison or similar. No availability guarantee per the Sury maintainer - re-check on every release refresh.

DB_MARIADB.label: MariaDB
DB_MARIADB.question: Install MariaDB?
DB_MARIADB.note: DB server engine. Shown together with DB_POSTGRESQL + DB_REDIS as a shared multi-select screen (group 'db' in grouped_prompts). phpMyAdmin follows implicitly (DB_PHPMYADMIN, type derived).

DB_POSTGRESQL.label: PostgreSQL
DB_POSTGRESQL.description: Object-relational DB server (web UI via Adminer addon)
DB_POSTGRESQL.question: Install PostgreSQL?

DB_REDIS.label: Redis
DB_REDIS.description: In-memory cache / key-value store (not a DB server)
DB_REDIS.question: Install Redis?

DB_MARIADB_VERSION.question: Which MariaDB version to install?
DB_MARIADB_VERSION.note: Only shown/asked when MariaDB was chosen (visible_if DB_MARIADB == true). 11.4 because of Magento 2.4.9 (Adobe-approved); 10.6 and older excluded (EOL 2026-07-06). __os__ is resolved at runtime.

DB_PHPMYADMIN.note: Implicit: installed without its own question as soon as MariaDB is chosen (value = DB_MARIADB).

MAIL_BLOCK_PRESENT.question: Install mail block (Dovecot mailboxes)?
MAIL_BLOCK_PRESENT.description: Determines whether a mail block exists at all. Normally not a user question - follows directly from the preset. Exception: under the 'custom' preset (default null) it is asked as a real question.
MAIL_BLOCK_PRESENT.note: When false (nomail): Dovecot is dropped entirely, Exim runs as a transactional-only MTA (no mailbox reception). When true: Dovecot is fixed on, no separate on/off toggle.

MAIL_WEBMAILER.question: Webmail client:
MAIL_WEBMAILER.note: Roundcube/SnappyMail are served architecturally via Panel-Caddy+Panel-PHP; the respective web server (nginx/Apache) only acts as a reverse proxy to the webmail subdomain. DKIM runs independently via the existing Exim+OpenSSL mechanism (inherited from HestiaCP upstream, not rspamd dkim_signing).

PANEL_PHP.note: Just a wrapper around a PHP instance for the panel itself. Uses os_single on mailonly, otherwise depends on PHP_MODE. reference_version = the reference version used by Panel-FPM and always fully installed (#272); single source of truth for the installer (PHP_VER) and the wizard checklist lock.

PANEL_EXIM.note: Always installed. The operating mode (full MTA with Dovecot integration vs. send-only) derives implicitly from MAIL_BLOCK_PRESENT, no separate question. DKIM signing is done granularly per domain via OpenSSL (existing HestiaCP mechanism), independent of rspamd.

ADDON_ADMINER.label: Adminer
ADDON_ADMINER.description: Web DB UI for PostgreSQL (vendored single-file). MySQL keeps phpMyAdmin.
ADDON_ADMINER.note: Only shown/preselected when PostgreSQL was chosen (visible_if DB_POSTGRESQL == true, default true) - hidden and thus not installed without PostgreSQL. Replaces the former DB_PGADMIN derivation (pgadmin4-web exists in no OS repo, phpPgAdmin dormant; #350). Vendored from share/adminer/ instead of an OS package (OS versions are CVE-prone). A full build can also do MySQL, but phpMyAdmin remains untouched as the MySQL default.

ADDON_COMPOSER.label: Composer
ADDON_COMPOSER.note: latest uses the upstream installer script (getcomposer.org) instead of the OS package for a more current version. Backlog: an h-update-sys-composer command to switch the source afterwards.

ADDON_CROWDSEC.label: CrowdSec
ADDON_CROWDSEC.note: Selectable independently of Fail2Ban, not an exclusive-or. Both can run in parallel.

ADDON_RSPAMD.label: rspamd
ADDON_RSPAMD.description: Spam filter incl. Bayes learning (mail block only; brings a small capped Redis)
ADDON_RSPAMD.note: Hidden on nomail (no mail block). Pure spam-filter component, NOT responsible for DKIM (see the PANEL_EXIM note). ALWAYS brings a Redis as the Bayes backend: without DB_REDIS hard-limited to 64 MB (share/rspamd/redis-rspamd-limit.conf), with DB_REDIS the general-purpose instance is shared unlimited. h-add-sys-redis must remove the limit (promote), h-delete-sys-redis must restore it instead of uninstalling (demote).

ADDON_CLAMAV.label: ClamAV
ADDON_CLAMAV.note: Hidden on nomail (no mail block). NEVER preselected - clamd holds the entire signature DB in RAM (~1-2 GB). h-add-sys-clamav sets ANTIVIRUS_SYSTEM=clamav (new mail domains scan by default, opt-out per domain) and arms the exim CLAMD macro ONLY once clamd answers on the socket. The macro uses malware=*/defer_ok = FAIL-OPEN: with a dead clamd, mail is accepted unscanned (availability over security), hence no arming without a live clamd. Delete = saved state (per-domain flags stay, reactivate on reinstall).

ADDON_DOCKER.label: Docker
ADDON_DOCKER.note: Shown in all presets, preset nowhere. Real resource footprint (RAM/disk), deliberately no automatic activation.

ADDON_FILEMANAGER.label: FileManager
ADDON_FILEMANAGER.note: Shown in all presets incl. mailonly, preset nowhere. TODO: dependencies on PHP version/mode still to be checked before production use.

ADDON_UTILITIES.label: Utilities
ADDON_UTILITIES.note: When enabled, the tools follow-up page opens (see the tools section below).

selection.question: Which utilities to install?
selection.note: restic: backend functions (v-backup-user-restic, v-backup-users-restic) already exist in the code, only the package install is missing - higher backlog priority than the other unset tools.

fasttrack.note: Takes only the component selection from the preset, skips all component questions. Hostname/port/username/email remain mandatory interactive prompts, no password parameter (see ADMIN_PASSWORD). 'custom' is not selectable in fasttrack mode.
fasttrack.status: Idea/backlog, not yet core work of this iteration

_open_questions: DKIM mechanism: confirmed - the existing HestiaCP Exim+OpenSSL path stays, no rspamd dkim_signing
nginx-lite for mailonly: check later whether a leaner nginx build without PHP-hosting modules suffices

Filesystem quota and resource limitation: deliberately not included in the manifest (maturity/bug history in the original upstream), backlog item: re-evaluate later and decide whether the associated h-* commands are removed or kept
